doc:
Defines one or more SMARTS-based rules for identifying atoms (i.e., a tuple of atoms) to edit or use to add new atoms. Rules are separated by newline characters (i.e., each rule is defined in a single line). In each line, each space-separated string (i.e., a word) is interpreted as following: each word is considered a single SMARTS until a word is found that corresponds to any keyword (see below). Once the first keyword is found no more words will be interpreted as SMARTS. There can be one, multi-atom SMARTS (e.g., [#1]-[#6]-[#8]-[*], [#6]-[#8]), or multiple single-atom SMARTS. The order of multiple SMARTS is retained and used to generate the ordered tuple of atom indexes. Recognized keywords are (case-insensitive):
--> 'remove': to request the removal of the matched atoms (mutually exclusive with other behavior-controlling keywords, like 'addAtom' and 'element').
 --> 'element': to request the mutation of the elemental symbol of the matched atoms (mutually exclusive with other behavior-controlling keywords, like 'addAtom' and 'remove'). To this end append the new elemental symbol (e.g., 'element: N').
 --> 'addAtom': to request the addition of a single atom (mutually exclusive with other behavior-controlling keywords, like 'element' and 'remove'). The value of this keyword must start with the elemental symbol of the new atom following by details about the relative position and bonding of such atom: '<el> <string_details>'. Where <string_details> may have the following syntax:
 -----> 'CENTROID' to add an atom in the position defined by the centroid of the matched atoms.
 -----> 'INTERNALCOORDS' to specify the position of the new atom relative to the atoms in the matched atom tuple. To this end this keyword should be followed by a string adhering to this syntax: '<idA> <valueA> [<idB> <valueB> [<idC> <valueC>]]' where <id...> are indexes in the matched tuple of atoms and <value...> are numerical values used together with the indexes to define internal coordinates in the order 'distance', 'angle', 'dihedral' (No second angle!).
 --> 'OnlyBonded': limits to matched tuples of atoms to those that are connected to form a path visiting the tuple in the order given by the order of the SMARTS. For example, a SMARTS-based rule for tuple '[#1] [#6] [#7]' may be limited to match only substructures where the H atom is connected with any bond to the C atom, which is connected with any bond to the N atom.
 --> 'GeometryConditions=type id0 id1 ... operator [, type id0 id1 ... operator, [...]]' to specify a list of geometry conditions that the tuples must satisfy. Multiple geometry conditions can be specified in a comma-separated list. Each geometry conditions is defined by the syntax 'type id0 id1 ... operator [values]': a space-separated list of strings consisting of the type (i.e., DISTANCE, ANGLE, DIHEDRAL), the list of indexes of the atoms involved in the condition (0-based index of the atom identified by the tuple, not index the overall atom list), the operator (either as string, i.e., CLOSE_TO, LESS_THAN, MORE_THAN, MAX, MIN, or ad symbol <, >, =), and any numerical value that might be needed by the operator (e.g., LESS_THAN/'<' and MORE_THAN/'>' require one reference value, CLOSE_TO/'=' may be used with one value or with two values, where the second is the tolerance value for considering two numbers sufficiently close to satisfy the condition).

Defines one or more (0-based) index-based rules for identifying atoms to edit or use to add new atoms. Rules are separated by newline characters (i.e., each rule is defined in a single line). In each line, each space-separated string (i.e., a word) is interpreted as following: each word is considered a single index until a word is found that corresponds to any keyword (see below). Once the first keyword is found no more words will be interpreted as an index. The order of the indexes is retained. Recognized keywords are (case-insensitive):
--> 'remove': to request the removal of the matched atoms (mutually exclusive with other behavior-controlling keywords, like 'addAtom' and 'element').
 'element' <newElementalSymbol>: to request the mutation of the elemental symbol of the matched atoms (mutually exclusive with other behavior-controlling keywords, like 'addAtom' and 'remove').
 --> 'addAtom': to request the addition od a single atom (mutually exclusive with other behavior-controlling keywords, like 'element' and 'remove'). The value of this keyword must start with the elemental symbol of the new atom following by details about the relative position and bonding of such atom: '<el> <string_details>'. Where <string_details> may have the following syntax:
 -----> 'CENTROID' to add an atom in the position defined by the centroid of the matched atoms.
 -----> 'INTERNALCOORDS' to specify the position of the new atom relative to the atoms in the matched atom tuple. To this end this keyword should be followed by a string adhering to this syntax: '<idA> <valueA> [<idB> <valueB> [<idC> <valueC>]]' where <id...> are indexes in the matched tuple of atoms and <value...> are numerical values used together with the indexes to define internal coordinates in the order 'distance', 'angle', 'dihedral' (No second angle!).
 --> 'OnlyBonded': limits to tuples of atoms to those that are connected to form a path visiting the tuple in the order given by the order of the SMARTS. For example, a SMARTS-based rule for tuple '[#1] [#6] [#7]' may be limited to match only substructures where the H atom is connected with any bond to the C atom, which is connected with any bond to the N atom.

tag: the importing of chemical objects (e.g., molecular geometries)
